scenarios.name: North Hills Family Medicine Associates
scenarios.description: Large 8-physician primary care group demonstrating rapid E/M coding improvement and gradual documentation enhancement. Starting denial rate 14.2%, target 8.1%. Key wins: Modifier 25 pattern resolved, medical necessity documentation improving, duplicate claims eliminated via system fix.

keyEvents.description: Baseline period begins - practice submitting ~800 claims/month to UPMC Health Plan
Missing Modifier 25 pattern detected - 47 denials in first month
Medical necessity documentation gaps identified for extended E/M visits
Preventive + problem-oriented visit coding overlap flagged
Billing manager views modifier 25 pattern, exports claims, tests corrections in Claim Lab
Billing team implements modifier 25 checklist for all E/M + procedure claims
Duplicate claim submissions detected - clearinghouse resubmission timing issue
Clearinghouse resubmission timer and duplicate detection rules configured
Lead physician reviews medical necessity documentation guidelines
Annual wellness visit frequency limit pattern detected
Modifier 25 pattern resolved - denial rate at 2.1% (down from 22%)
Coding team reviews preventive vs problem visit split-billing rules
Documentation template updated with medical necessity prompts
Current state - overall denial rate reduced from 14.2% to 8.1%

patterns.title: Missing Modifier 25 on E/M with Same-Day Minor Procedure
patterns.description: When an E/M visit (99213-99215) is billed on the same day as a minor procedure, modifier 25 is required to indicate the E/M was a significant, separately identifiable service. This practice was consistently omitting modifier 25, causing automatic denials. High appeal rate (65%) with 73% overturn rate confirms these are pure administrative waste.
patterns.denialReason: Modifier-25 required for significant, separately identifiable E/M service on same date as procedure

actionsRecorded.description: Billing team trained on modifier 25 requirements
Modifier 25 checklist added to EHR workflow
Standard operating procedure for E/M + procedure claims

shortTerm.description: Resubmit denied claims from last 90 days with modifier 25 appended and supporting documentation

longTerm.description: Embed modifier 25 logic into E/M billing workflow so claims are never submitted without it when a same-day procedure exists
steps: Add modifier 25 auto-prompt in EHR when procedure + E/M on same date
Train all billing staff on modifier 25 criteria
Implement monthly audit of E/M + procedure same-day claims
Create quick-reference card for front desk/coders

patterns.title: Insufficient Medical Necessity Documentation for Extended E/M Visits
patterns.description: Extended office visits (99215, 99205) denied for insufficient medical necessity documentation. Visits are clinically appropriate but documentation doesn't clearly articulate why the higher-level service was required. Requires physician behavior change - improvement is gradual. 52% appeal rate, 48% overturn.
patterns.denialReason: Documentation does not support medical necessity for the level of E/M service billed

actionsRecorded.description: Provider meeting on medical necessity documentation standards
Documentation template updated with medical necessity prompts

shortTerm.description: Append additional clinical documentation and resubmit with medical necessity justification

longTerm.description: Update clinical documentation templates to prompt medical necessity language for extended E/M visits
steps: Add medical necessity prompts to EHR visit templates for 99215/99205
Conduct physician education sessions on documentation requirements
Implement pre-submission documentation review for high-level E/M
Schedule quarterly chart audits focused on extended E/M visits

patterns.title: Preventive Visit and Problem-Oriented Visit Coding Overlap
patterns.description: When a patient presents for a preventive wellness visit and a problem is also addressed, split-billing with modifier 25 on the problem-oriented E/M is required. Many claims submitted with both codes but without proper modifier or documentation.
patterns.denialReason: Preventive and problem-oriented E/M services billed same day without appropriate modifier or documentation of distinct service

actionsRecorded.description: Coding team reviewed preventive vs problem visit split-billing rules

shortTerm.description: Resubmit with modifier 25 and documentation of distinct problem-oriented service

longTerm.description: Establish split-billing workflow for wellness visits where problems are also addressed
steps: Create EHR alert when wellness + E/M codes on same encounter
Train physicians on documenting separate clinical decision-making
Add modifier 25 auto-prompt for dual-purpose visits

patterns.title: Duplicate Claim Submissions from Clearinghouse Resubmission Logic
patterns.description: Practice management system was automatically resubmitting claims before receiving payer response, generating duplicate submissions. Pure system configuration fix - once the resubmission timer was adjusted and duplicate detection enabled, the problem nearly disappeared.
patterns.denialReason: Duplicate claim - previously submitted claim for same service, same date, same provider

actionsRecorded.description: Clearinghouse resubmission timer adjusted from 24h to 72h
Duplicate detection rule enabled in practice management system

shortTerm.description: No resubmission needed - these are true duplicates. Write off denied duplicates.

longTerm.description: Configure clearinghouse and PMS to prevent duplicate submissions
steps: Extend clearinghouse resubmission wait timer to 72 hours
Enable duplicate claim detection in practice management system
Set up daily report flagging potential duplicate submissions

patterns.title: Annual Wellness Visit Frequency Limit - Multiple Visits in Benefit Year
patterns.description: Patients occasionally have two annual wellness visits billed in the same benefit year. These denials are legitimate and rarely appealed. The practice now checks eligibility but cannot eliminate all instances.
patterns.denialReason: Frequency limit exceeded - annual wellness visit already billed within benefit year

shortTerm.description: Cannot resubmit - legitimate frequency limit denials

longTerm.description: Improve front-desk eligibility verification to catch frequency limits before scheduling
steps: Check preventive visit history at scheduling
Add benefit year wellness visit flag to patient chart
Train schedulers to verify wellness visit eligibility

scenarios.name: South Hills Orthopedics & Sports Medicine
scenarios.description: Mid-size orthopedic group with surgical and non-surgical providers. Highest-dollar recovery scenario due to surgical claim values. Key patterns: bilateral modifier 50, injection bundling with E/M, NCCI edit violations, prior auth for imaging. Starting denial rate 16.8%, target 9.4%.

keyEvents.description: Baseline period - practice submitting ~450 claims/month, high surgical mix
Missing bilateral modifier (50) on bilateral knee/shoulder procedures
E/M billed same day as injection without modifier 25
Prior authorization missing for MRI and advanced imaging
Lead surgeon reviews bilateral modifier rules, shares with partners
Billing department adds bilateral check to surgical claim review
NCCI bundling edit violations - distinct procedure modifier 59 needed
Pain management physician tests injection + E/M corrections in Claim Lab
Front desk begins checking prior auth status before scheduling imaging
Bilateral modifier pattern resolved - near zero denials
Physical therapy frequency limit exceeded for some patients
Injection bundling SOP distributed to all providers
Global surgery period - E/M billed during post-op window
Current state - denial rate reduced from 16.8% to 9.4%

patterns.title: Missing Bilateral Modifier (50) on Bilateral Joint Procedures
patterns.description: When bilateral procedures (both knees, both shoulders) are performed, modifier 50 is required. Without it, the second side is denied as a duplicate. High-dollar surgical claims ($6K+ each) - massive financial impact. 71% appeal rate, 80% overturn. Pure waste.
patterns.denialReason: Bilateral modifier (50) required for bilateral joint procedure - second side denied as potential duplicate

actionsRecorded.description: Surgeons briefed on bilateral modifier requirements
Bilateral check added to surgical claim template

shortTerm.description: Resubmit denied bilateral claims with modifier 50

longTerm.description: Embed bilateral modifier logic into surgical claim workflow
steps: Add bilateral flag to surgery scheduling form
Auto-populate modifier 50 when bilateral procedure scheduled
Monthly audit of bilateral surgical claims

patterns.title: E/M Visit Billed Same Day as Joint Injection Without Modifier 25
patterns.description: When a physician performs an evaluation AND a joint injection on the same day, the E/M requires modifier 25. Pain management and sports medicine providers frequently omit this.
patterns.denialReason: E/M service bundled with injection procedure - modifier 25 required for separate E/M

actionsRecorded.description: Injection + E/M billing SOP distributed

shortTerm.description: Resubmit with modifier 25 and documentation of separate E/M

longTerm.description: Train all injection providers on modifier 25 requirements
steps: Create injection visit billing SOP
Add modifier 25 prompt to injection encounter template
Quarterly audit of injection + E/M claims

patterns.title: Prior Authorization Missing for Advanced Imaging (MRI/CT)
patterns.description: MRI and CT imaging for orthopedic conditions requires prior authorization from UPMC Health Plan. The front desk ordering process doesn't consistently capture PA before the study is performed.
patterns.denialReason: Prior authorization required for advanced imaging - no authorization on file at time of service

actionsRecorded.description: Front desk PA check added before imaging scheduling

shortTerm.description: Request retroactive PA where clinical documentation supports medical necessity

longTerm.description: Redesign imaging ordering workflow to require PA confirmation before study
steps: Add PA verification step to imaging order workflow
Create PA tracking dashboard for pending authorizations
Train front desk on payer-specific PA requirements

patterns.title: NCCI Bundling Edit Violations - Modifier 59 Required for Distinct Procedures
patterns.description: When multiple surgical procedures are performed in the same session, NCCI edits may bundle them. Modifier 59 (or X-modifiers) is required to indicate the procedures were distinct and separate.
patterns.denialReason: NCCI bundling edit - modifier 59/XE/XS/XP/XU required to indicate distinct procedural service

actionsRecorded.description: Coders trained on NCCI edit pairs and modifier 59 usage

shortTerm.description: Resubmit with appropriate modifier 59/X-modifier and operative note documentation

longTerm.description: Implement NCCI edit check in pre-submission claim scrubbing
steps: Add NCCI edit pair reference to coding tools
Train coders on modifier 59 vs X-modifier usage
Integrate CCI edit check into claim submission workflow

patterns.title: Physical Therapy Visit Frequency Exceeds Plan Benefit Limit
patterns.description: Post-surgical patients sometimes exceed their plan's PT visit limit. Legitimate denials. Practice now checks remaining visits but cannot eliminate all occurrences.
patterns.denialReason: Physical therapy visit frequency exceeds plan benefit limit for current benefit period

shortTerm.description: Cannot resubmit - legitimate benefit limit exceeded

longTerm.description: Check remaining PT visits before scheduling
steps: Verify remaining PT visits at each appointment
Alert PT when patient approaching visit limit
Discuss self-pay options with patient

patterns.title: E/M Visit During Global Surgery Post-Operative Period
patterns.description: E/M visits billed during the global surgery period (10 or 90 day post-op window) are denied because follow-up care is included in the surgical fee. Some visits are legitimately separate (new problem, modifier 24).
patterns.denialReason: E/M service billed during global surgery post-operative period - included in surgical package

shortTerm.description: For visits addressing new/unrelated problems, resubmit with modifier 24

longTerm.description: Educate surgeons on global period rules and modifier 24 criteria
steps: Create global period quick-reference for common surgical CPT codes
Flag scheduled post-op visits in EMR during global period
Train surgeons on modifier 24 criteria for unrelated problems

scenarios.name: Allegheny Cardiovascular Associates
scenarios.description: Cardiology practice with highest dollar recovery due to high-value procedural claims. Key patterns: cardiac diagnostic bundling, cath lab prior auth, stress test medical necessity, POS errors. Starting denial rate 15.4%, target 10.2%.

keyEvents.description: Baseline period - ~320 claims/month, heavy diagnostic and interventional mix
Cardiac diagnostic test bundling violations detected
Prior auth missing for cardiac catheterization
Medical necessity documentation insufficient for stress testing
Cardiology coding specialist deep-dives bundling rules in portal
Bundling edit check integrated into billing workflow
Place of service errors on outpatient cardiac procedures
Office manager overhauls PA tracking process for cath lab
Diagnostic bundling pattern resolved
Experimental/investigational denial on newer cardiac devices
PA compliance rate reaches 94% for cath procedures
Current state - denial rate from 15.4% to 10.2%

patterns.title: Cardiac Diagnostic Test Bundling - Echo + EKG + Stress Test Same Day
patterns.description: Multiple cardiac diagnostic tests performed same day triggering CCI bundling edits. Practice was billing echo, EKG, and stress test components separately without proper modifier usage. 61% appeal rate, 68% overturn - legitimate services just incorrectly unbundled.
patterns.denialReason: CCI bundling edit - cardiac diagnostic components require appropriate modifier or are inclusive of primary procedure

actionsRecorded.description: Coding team trained on cardiac bundling rules and CCI edits
Pre-submission edit check for cardiac bundling added
Same-day cardiac testing billing SOP created

shortTerm.description: Resubmit with appropriate modifiers and documentation of distinct services

longTerm.description: Implement pre-submission CCI edit checking for all cardiac diagnostic claims
steps: Integrate CCI edit engine into claim scrubbing workflow
Create cardiac diagnostic billing quick-reference
Quarterly audit of same-day cardiac testing claims

patterns.title: Prior Authorization Missing for Cardiac Catheterization
patterns.description: Cardiac cath procedures ($8K+ per case) require prior authorization. Cath lab scheduling workflow didn't consistently verify PA status. High-dollar denials drive aggressive appeals (75% appeal rate, 67% overturn).
patterns.denialReason: Prior authorization required for cardiac catheterization - no authorization on file

actionsRecorded.description: PA tracking system implemented for all scheduled cath procedures
EHR alerts for PA requirement on cath orders

shortTerm.description: Request retroactive PA with clinical documentation for recent denials

longTerm.description: Require PA verification before any cath procedure is scheduled
steps: Add PA hold to cath lab scheduling system
Create PA verification checklist for cath pre-procedure workflow
Weekly PA status report for upcoming cath procedures

patterns.title: Medical Necessity Documentation Insufficient for Cardiac Stress Testing
patterns.description: Stress tests denied for insufficient documentation of medical necessity - ordering indication not clearly linked to cardiac risk factors or symptoms. Moderate churn with mixed appeal success.
patterns.denialReason: Medical necessity documentation insufficient - clinical indication for cardiac stress testing not adequately documented

actionsRecorded.description: Physicians reviewed medical necessity criteria for stress testing

shortTerm.description: Append clinical documentation linking symptoms/risk factors to stress test indication

longTerm.description: Add medical necessity documentation prompts to stress test ordering workflow
steps: Create stress test order template with required clinical fields
Add ACC/AHA appropriate use criteria reference to ordering system
Conduct physician education on documentation standards

patterns.title: Incorrect Place of Service Code for Outpatient Cardiac Procedures
patterns.description: Some outpatient cardiac procedures billed with incorrect POS code. System configuration issue - once templates were corrected, volume dropped significantly.
patterns.denialReason: Place of service does not match expected setting for billed procedure

actionsRecorded.description: POS codes updated in practice management system templates

shortTerm.description: Resubmit with corrected POS code

longTerm.description: Correct POS defaults in practice management system
steps: Audit all procedure templates for correct POS codes
Update PMS templates for office vs facility settings
Add POS validation to pre-submission claim check

patterns.title: Experimental/Investigational Denial on Newer Cardiac Device Procedures
patterns.description: Newer cardiac devices (leadless pacemakers, subcutaneous ICDs) occasionally denied as experimental/investigational despite FDA approval. Sticky denial - plan policy is clear.
patterns.denialReason: Procedure classified as experimental/investigational per plan policy - not a covered benefit

shortTerm.description: Appeal with peer-reviewed literature and FDA clearance documentation - low success rate

longTerm.description: Monitor plan policy updates - devices may be reclassified as covered
steps: Track plan medical policy updates quarterly
Build clinical evidence portfolio for future appeals
Coordinate with device manufacturer for payer education

scenarios.name: Cranberry Gastroenterology & Endoscopy Center
scenarios.description: GI practice resolving screening-to-diagnostic colonoscopy coding issues, endoscopy prior auth gaps, and anesthesia bundling. Starting denial rate 13.1%, target 7.8%. The screening-to-diagnostic conversion pattern is a classic GI coding challenge that the portal resolves quickly.

keyEvents.description: Baseline period - ~280 claims/month, heavy endoscopy volume
Screening-to-diagnostic colonoscopy coding errors detected
Prior auth gaps on upper endoscopy (EGD)
Anesthesia bundling errors on colonoscopy claims
GI physicians review screening vs diagnostic criteria in portal
Coding team implements polyp-detection coding workflow
Billing manager tests anesthesia bundling corrections in Claim Lab
Surveillance colonoscopy frequency limit - too early follow-up
Screening-to-diagnostic coding pattern largely resolved
Pathology specimen handling codes denied - bundling with endoscopy
PA compliance improves to 91% for EGD procedures
Current state - denial rate from 13.1% to 7.8%

patterns.title: Screening-to-Diagnostic Colonoscopy Coding Error - Polyp Found, Code Not Updated
patterns.description: When a polyp is found during a screening colonoscopy, the procedure code should be updated from screening (G0121/G0105) to therapeutic (45380-45385). Practice was not consistently making the conversion. Classic GI coding issue - 67% appeal rate, 71% overturn.
patterns.denialReason: Screening colonoscopy code submitted with therapeutic intervention - code should reflect diagnostic/therapeutic procedure

actionsRecorded.description: All GI physicians trained on screening-to-diagnostic code conversion
Polyp detection triggers automatic code conversion in endoscopy report
Endoscopy coding quick-reference card created

shortTerm.description: Resubmit with corrected procedure codes reflecting therapeutic intervention

longTerm.description: Automate code conversion in endoscopy reporting system when findings indicate therapeutic procedure
steps: Link pathology findings to automatic code conversion
Create endoscopy coding decision tree
Quarterly audit of screening vs diagnostic coding accuracy

patterns.title: Prior Authorization Missing for Upper Endoscopy (EGD)
patterns.description: EGD procedures require prior auth. Scheduling workflow doesn't consistently verify PA before the procedure date.
patterns.denialReason: Prior authorization required for upper endoscopy - no authorization on file at time of service

actionsRecorded.description: PA tracking added to endoscopy scheduling workflow

shortTerm.description: Request retroactive PA for denied EGD claims with clinical documentation

longTerm.description: Require PA confirmation before any EGD is scheduled
steps: Add PA verification to endoscopy scheduling checklist
Create PA tracking dashboard
Weekly report on upcoming procedures without PA

patterns.title: Anesthesia Service Bundling with Endoscopy - Separate Billing Denied
patterns.description: Monitored anesthesia care (MAC) billed separately from endoscopy procedure triggering bundling denials. Specific modifier and documentation requirements apply.
patterns.denialReason: Anesthesia service bundled with endoscopy procedure - separate billing requires appropriate modifier and documentation

actionsRecorded.description: Anesthesia billing rules reviewed with CRNA team

shortTerm.description: Resubmit with appropriate anesthesia modifier and MAC documentation

longTerm.description: Standardize anesthesia coding for all endoscopy procedures
steps: Create anesthesia billing template for endoscopy
Add MAC documentation checklist to anesthesia record
Quarterly audit of endoscopy + anesthesia claims

patterns.title: Surveillance Colonoscopy Scheduled Before Recommended Interval
patterns.description: Patients scheduled for surveillance colonoscopy before guideline-recommended interval. Legitimate denials - plan follows established surveillance guidelines.
patterns.denialReason: Surveillance colonoscopy frequency exceeds guideline-recommended interval for patient risk profile

shortTerm.description: Cannot resubmit - guideline-based frequency limit

longTerm.description: Check colonoscopy history and guideline interval at scheduling
steps: Verify last colonoscopy date and findings before scheduling
Create surveillance interval reference based on polyp risk stratification
Alert scheduler when interval is shorter than recommended

patterns.title: Pathology Specimen Handling Codes Bundled with Endoscopy
patterns.description: Pathology specimen handling and processing codes billed separately are being bundled with the endoscopy procedure code. Moderate churn - some are appropriately separate but documentation doesn't support it.
patterns.denialReason: Pathology specimen handling included in endoscopy procedure - separate billing not supported

shortTerm.description: Review denied pathology claims and resubmit with documentation supporting separate service

longTerm.description: Clarify which pathology services can be billed separately from endoscopy
steps: Create pathology billing reference for endoscopy procedures
Coordinate with pathology lab on bundling rules

scenarios.name: Oakland Behavioral Health & Psychiatry Associates
scenarios.description: Behavioral health practice addressing telehealth POS coding (fastest fix - system config), session time documentation, IOP prior auth, and psychotherapy add-on coding. High telehealth mix (55%) makes POS pattern an easy win. Starting denial rate 12.6%, target 7.2%.

keyEvents.description: Baseline period - ~360 claims/month, 55% telehealth mix
Telehealth POS code errors - using POS 11 instead of POS 10/02
Session time documentation insufficient for time-based psychotherapy billing
Prior auth missing for intensive outpatient program (IOP) sessions
Practice manager reviews telehealth POS requirements - quick fix identified
EHR telehealth templates updated with correct POS 10 and modifier 95
Telehealth POS pattern resolved - system configuration fix
Concurrent individual and group therapy same day - benefit limitation
Psychiatrists review time-based documentation requirements
Session time tracking template added to clinical notes
Psychotherapy add-on code (90833) denied without qualifying E/M
IOP PA process improved - authorization secured before first session
Current state - denial rate from 12.6% to 7.2%

patterns.title: Telehealth Place of Service Code Error - POS 11 Used Instead of POS 10/02
patterns.description: With 55% of visits via telehealth, incorrect POS code generated significant denial volume. EHR templates defaulted to POS 11 (office). Simple system config fix resolved it almost immediately. 54% appeal rate, 70% overturn - clear administrative waste.
patterns.denialReason: Place of service code does not match telehealth service - POS 10 or 02 required for telehealth encounters

actionsRecorded.description: EHR telehealth encounter templates updated with POS 10 and modifier 95
Telehealth appointment type auto-populates correct POS code

shortTerm.description: Resubmit denied telehealth claims with corrected POS 10 and modifier 95

longTerm.description: Configure EHR to automatically set correct POS for telehealth encounters
steps: Update all telehealth encounter templates with POS 10
Add modifier 95 auto-populate for synchronous telehealth
Create telehealth billing compliance checklist

patterns.title: Session Time Documentation Insufficient for Time-Based Psychotherapy Billing
patterns.description: Time-based psychotherapy codes (90834 = 38-52 min, 90837 = 53+ min) require documentation of actual session time. Many notes lack specific start/stop times, triggering denials. Requires physician behavior change.
patterns.denialReason: Session time documentation does not support the level of psychotherapy service billed

actionsRecorded.description: Session time tracking template added to clinical notes
All providers trained on time-based documentation requirements

shortTerm.description: Addend notes with session times and resubmit

longTerm.description: Add mandatory time fields to psychotherapy note templates
steps: Add session start/stop time fields to all therapy note templates
Create documentation guide for time-based psychotherapy coding
Implement monthly chart audit for session time documentation

patterns.title: Prior Authorization Missing for Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)
patterns.description: IOP sessions require prior authorization. New IOP patients sometimes started treatment before PA was secured.
patterns.denialReason: Prior authorization required for intensive outpatient program - no authorization on file

actionsRecorded.description: IOP intake process updated to require PA before first session
Admissions team trained on PA timeline requirements

shortTerm.description: Request retroactive PA for IOP sessions with clinical documentation

longTerm.description: Require PA confirmation before first IOP session
steps: Add PA hold to IOP admission workflow
Create PA tracking for all IOP patients
Weekly PA status review for active IOP participants

patterns.title: Concurrent Individual and Group Therapy Same Day - Benefit Limitation
patterns.description: Some patients receiving both individual and group therapy on the same day. Plan policy limits coverage to one therapy type per day. Legitimate denials - rarely appealed.
patterns.denialReason: Benefit limitation - individual and group therapy not covered on same date of service

shortTerm.description: Cannot resubmit - legitimate benefit limitation

longTerm.description: Schedule individual and group therapy on different days
steps: Update scheduling to prevent same-day individual + group therapy
Educate therapists on benefit limitations for concurrent services

patterns.title: Psychotherapy Add-On Code (90833) Denied Without Qualifying E/M Service
patterns.description: The add-on psychotherapy code 90833 must be billed with a qualifying primary E/M service (99213-99215). Some claims submitted 90833 alone or with non-qualifying primary codes.
patterns.denialReason: Psychotherapy add-on code 90833 requires a qualifying primary E/M service on the same date

shortTerm.description: Resubmit with qualifying primary E/M code

longTerm.description: Educate psychiatrists on add-on code requirements and billing pairs
steps: Create add-on code quick-reference for psychiatry billing
Add validation rule preventing 90833 submission without qualifying E/M
